source.title: Forward and reverse hippocampal place-cell sequences during ripples

confidence_rationale: Canonical demonstration of two distinct SWR sampling modes -- forward sequences pre-run, reverse sequences post-run -- implying that the seed-state distribution is mode-dependent. This constrains MECH-285 architecturally: any single staleness-weighted sampler is probably insufficient; the sleep consumer likely inherits distinct forward/reverse priorities with different seeding rules. Good methods, directly relevant to the priority-shape question, but does not itself manipulate staleness or salience.
failure_signatures: Seed-state distribution differs between forward-sequence replay (occurring in anticipation of a run) and reverse-sequence replay (occurring at end of a run); a single unified sampler cannot reproduce both.
Vector distances between place fields are preserved in the temporal structure of both forward and reverse sequences, indicating the seed distribution encodes spatial topology regardless of direction.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal place-cell spike sequences recur in reverse order at run end and in forward order in anticipation of a run, coinciding with sharp waves; the temporal structure of these sequences preserves spatial relations between place fields.
mapping.ree_translation: For MECH-285 the architectural implication is that the seed distribution is not mode-agnostic. Forward and reverse sequences are triggered at different behavioural moments and seed from different states: forward replay seeds near the animal's current position and runs toward the goal; reverse replay seeds at the goal and runs back. The in-silico sampler must therefore be mode-aware -- a MECH-285 priority weight may need to be factored per-mode. Staleness-weighted broad coverage may interact differently with forward vs reverse SWR triggers.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Study is awake, not sleep. Run-triggered vs run-terminated SWR dynamics may not cleanly map onto sleep-phase modes (NREM-SWR vs REM content is a separate division). The paper does not manipulate staleness, reward, or salience; it describes the dual-mode phenomenology without adjudicating what drives seed selection within each mode.
mapping.source_context: Rat hippocampus; elevated track runs with defined start/end points; awake recordings spanning pre-run, during-run, and post-run epochs.

confidence_components.notes: Foundational phenomenological result. Mapping fidelity is moderate because the paper describes mode structure but does not directly bear on MECH-285's specific priority-shape or timing questions; it constrains the architecture (two-mode sampler likely needed) without settling any of the verdicts.
